context.description: The page reflects on whether consciousness can access intrinsic brain capacities such as spatial perception, out-of-body experiences, and spontaneous visions without external substances. It then introduces a new section on how nutrition may affect the interface between body and awareness, including ketosis, carbohydrate metabolism, and brain fog.
normalization_notes: Page discusses consciousness, perception, out-of-body experiences, and the possible influence of nutrition on the body-consciousness interface.
Chapter heading translated from German and preserved as visible section text.

fields.section_heading: How much influence does nutrition have on the connection between machine and awareness?

rows.heading_text: How much influence does nutrition have on the connection between machine and awareness?
rows.text_content: How much influence does nutrition have on the connection between machine and awareness?

rows.paragraph_text: Spatial perception not bound to a pair of eyes is discussed as something that can occur without external substances. The text asks whether out-of-body experiences, spontaneous visions, and other anomalous perceptions reflect an inherent brain capacity that consciousness cannot normally access.
rows.text_content: Spatial perception not bound to a pair of eyes is discussed as something that can occur without external substances. The text asks whether out-of-body experiences, spontaneous visions, and other anomalous perceptions reflect an inherent brain capacity that consciousness cannot normally access.

rows.paragraph_text: Nutrition is presented as an overlooked factor shaping the interface between body and awareness, especially through changes in metabolism and their effect on perception, cognitive performance, and information distortion.
rows.text_content: Nutrition is presented as an overlooked factor shaping the interface between body and awareness, especially through changes in metabolism and their effect on perception, cognitive performance, and information distortion.

rows.paragraph_text: Brain fog is introduced as a common example of impaired thinking and dulled stimulus perception, used to illustrate reduced overall functioning.
rows.text_content: Brain fog is introduced as a common example of impaired thinking and dulled stimulus perception, used to illustrate reduced overall functioning.

content.free_text: book_page nonfiction_philosophy philosophy_of_consciousness Work title: Bewusstseinsreisen - Version 2. Section on the influence of nutrition on awareness and the body-consciousness interface. Topics include out-of-body experiences, spontaneous visions, ketosis and carbohydrate metabolism, and brain fog.
